findings.title: Known compromised Axios release
findings.remediation: Remove the compromised Axios release, clear regenerated dependency artifacts, rotate exposed secrets, and reinstall from a trusted lockfile.

findings.title: Workflow uses pull_request_target
findings.remediation: Use pull_request for untrusted code, or split privileged release automation into a separate trusted workflow.

findings.title: Workflow uses a non-deterministic install command
findings.evidence: install command without frozen lockfile or hash enforcement
findings.remediation: Use npm ci, yarn install --immutable, pnpm install --frozen-lockfile, bun ci, bun install --frozen-lockfile, or pip install --require-hashes.

findings.title: Python .pth file executes code during interpreter startup
findings.remediation: Remove unexpected .pth executable code and rebuild the virtual environment from trusted, hashed requirements.

findings.title: Requirements file uses an extra package index
findings.remediation: Review package index trust boundaries. Remove extra indexes unless required, or isolate private indexes with hash-locked requirements.

resolvedFindings.title: Workflow action is not pinned to a full commit SHA
resolvedFindings.remediation: Pin third-party actions to a reviewed 40-character commit SHA.
